name: writing-design
description: Use to DESIGN or shape a NEW piece of writing before or while drafting — structure an argument or memo (PRD, RFC, recommendation), organize a task-oriented technical doc or explainer, get a first pass down without stalling, or give a talk, pitch, launch post a persuasive arc. Triggers on 'help me outline this RFC', 'draft this announcement', 'make this pitch land', 'structure this doc before I write it'.

inspired_by.name: The Pyramid Principle
inspired_by.author: Barbara Minto
inspired_by.contribution: Lead with the answer (top-down); the Situation-Complication-Question-Answer intro; group support mutually-exclusive and collectively-exhaustive.

inspired_by.name: Writing Software Documentation: A Task-Oriented Approach
inspired_by.author: Thomas Barker
inspired_by.contribution: Organize by what the user is trying to accomplish, not by features; audience and task analysis; decompose into ordered one-action steps; write minimally.

inspired_by.name: Bird by Bird
inspired_by.author: Anne Lamott
inspired_by.contribution: Permission to write a deliberately rough first pass; shrink the task to the next small unit; separate generating from editing.

inspired_by.name: On Writing Well
inspired_by.author: William Zinsser
inspired_by.contribution: Strip clutter; plain words over inflated ones; clarity is manufactured in revision.

inspired_by.name: The Sense of Style
inspired_by.author: Steven Pinker
inspired_by.contribution: Direct concrete style; the expert blind spot; order given-information before new.

inspired_by.name: Made to Stick
inspired_by.author: Chip Heath & Dan Heath
inspired_by.contribution: The six-part stickiness checklist (simple, unexpected, concrete, credible, emotional, story).

inspired_by.name: Resonate
inspired_by.author: Nancy Duarte
inspired_by.contribution: Oscillate between what-is and what-could-be; plant one memorable peak; cast the audience as hero.

inspired_by.name: Building a StoryBrand
inspired_by.author: Donald Miller
inspired_by.contribution: The audience is the hero and you are the guide; compress to a one-liner; the few-second stranger test.

inspired_by.name: Story
inspired_by.author: Robert McKee
inspired_by.contribution: The gap between expectation and result; the turning point where a value flips as the spine of a section.

inspired_by.name: The Anatomy of Story
inspired_by.author: John Truby
inspired_by.contribution: The throughline: the visible goal versus the deeper need it actually addresses.

inspired_by.name: Wired for Story
inspired_by.author: Lisa Cron
inspired_by.contribution: Open on a concrete problem and a goal in a worsening situation (cognitive framing, used as a lens not as evidence).

inspired_by.name: The Hero with a Thousand Faces
inspired_by.author: Joseph Campbell
inspired_by.contribution: The departure-initiation-return arc for a transformation narrative (generic arc only; the staged template carries known bias).

inspired_by.name: Poetics
inspired_by.author: Aristotle
inspired_by.contribution: Causal necessity over episodic listing; reversal and recognition; unity of action (public domain).

inspired_by.name: Save the Cat!
inspired_by.author: Blake Snyder
inspired_by.contribution: Pacing and sequencing logic — setup, catalyst, escalation, low point, resolution (principle only, not the named beat sheet).

inspired_by.name: And, But, Therefore (ABT)
inspired_by.author: Randy Olson
inspired_by.contribution: Compress any pitch to one sentence: context AND context, BUT tension, THEREFORE action.

inspired_by.name: The Story Spine
inspired_by.author: Kenn Adams
inspired_by.contribution: A five-line setup-disruption-because-of-that-resolution skeleton for a narrative or change story.

metadata.catalog_summary: Structure, draft, or add a persuasive arc to a new piece of writing — argument/memo, technical doc, talk/pitch, narrative, or general prose — routed by intent (structure/draft/persuade) × genre.
